disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bleeding stops promptly with correct pressure (lean forward, pinch soft cartilage); no continuous flow down the throat when compressed
trigger_question_examples: When you lean forward and firmly pinch the soft part of your nose for 10–15 minutes, does it stop?
With that pressure, is blood still running down the back of your throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I lean forward and pinch the soft part firmly it does slow and stop, and nothing's running down my throat then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No anticoagulant/antiplatelet use, no bleeding disorder, no easy bruising/petechiae
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any blood thinners or aspirin, or do you have a bleeding disorder?
Any easy bruising or bleeding from small cuts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood thinners or aspirin, no bleeding problems, and I don't bruise easily.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No true faintness on standing, no large-volume vomiting of blood; distress is anxiety-driven
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint or about to pass out when you stand, or is it anxiety?
Have you vomited a large amount of blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit lightheaded but I think it's nerves — I don't nearly pass out standing, and I haven't vomited blood.
